channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: FA Cup stunner: Liverpool is knocked out by second-tier Plymouth in fourth round
item.description: Premier League leader Liverpool was knocked out of the FA Cup by second-tier struggler Plymouth after losing 1-0 in a stunning fourth-round upset on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bucks announce Giannis Antetokounmpo out of All-Star Game with injury
item.description: Milwaukee forward Giannis Antetokounmpo will not play in next weekend's All-Star Game because of a strained left calf, meaning NBA Commissioner Adam Silver will have to pick a replacement.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Randy Moss makes an emotional return to the ESPN set following his cancer treatment
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Hall of Famer Randy Moss made an emotional return to ESPN's coverage of the Super Bowl on Sunday, two months after he took a leave from his job to get treated for cancer. Moss was back on ESPN's “Sunday NFL Countdown” before Kansas City took on Philadelphia in the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tiger Woods will play in Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines
item.description: Tiger Woods, the host of The Genesis Invitational and an 82-time PGA Tour winner, will play at Torrey Pines Golf Course this week.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl commercials rely on comedy and nostalgia to avoid potential missteps
item.description: This year’s Super Bowl commercials are going for laughs, with advertisers betting that viewers need a diversion from weighty matters.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Mae Anderson And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Heat officially welcome Wiggins, Mitchell and Anderson to the team after last week's Butler trade
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Things rarely happen in the NBA at 8:57 a.m. on a Sunday. But that was the moment when Miami's newest acquisitions — Andrew Wiggins, Davion Mitchell and Kyle Anderson — walked into the Heat interview room for the first time. The reason was simple: There's a lot of work to do.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Swiss skier Franjo von Allmen wins downhill gold at world championships
item.description: SAALBACH-HINTERGLEMM, Austria (AP) — Franjo von Allmen won gold in the downhill at the Alpine skiing world championships on Sunday as the Swiss team swept the men's speed events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jimmy Butler gets off to a good start with Warriors by scoring 25 points against Bulls in his debut
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Jimmy Butler drew the familiar “oohs” and “aahs” from the Chicago crowd when he cut backdoor for an alley-oop dunk in the opening minute. He had enough left to take over during Golden State's decisive run with Stephen Curry on the sideline.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wanna get away? Sharks blasted by Dallas Stars in final game before break
item.description: NHL: San Jose Sharks outclassed by Dallas Stars, enter 4 Nations Face-Off break having lost 10 of 11 games.
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: He's back, baby! ESPN's Dick Vitale makes return to commentating following 4th bout with cancer
item.description: ESPN’s Dick Vitale received a standing ovation Saturday night at Clemson’s Littlejohn Coliseum in his first game back as a commentator since recovering from his fourth bout with cancer.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patrick Mahomes' grandfather reportedly 'hanging on' in hospice care to watch grandson play in Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Patrick Mahomes' mother said her father is "hanging on," hoping to see the quarterback lead the Chiefs to victory over the Eagles in Sunday's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles activate Brandon Graham off injured reserve before Super Bowl LIX
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles activated defensive end Brandon Graham off injured reserve ahead of their matchup against the Kansas City Chiefs in Sunday's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ben Simmons waived by the Nets after the team and former No. 1 pick agree to a buyout
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ben Simmons was waived by the Brooklyn Nets on Saturday after the team agreed to a buyout with the last remaining link to a failed era of building around All-Stars. The No.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles' path to the Super Bowl was paved by the prowess of their offensive line
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles' offensive line could prove to be a pivotal factor in the outcome of their Super Bowl matchup with the Kansas City Chiefs.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vucevic and the Bulls take on Curry and the Warriors
item.description: Golden State Warriors (25-25, 11th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (22-30, 10th in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Bulls host the Golden State Warriors in out-of-conference play on Saturday. The Bulls are 10-16 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blackhawks visit the Blues after Donato's 2-goal performance
item.description: Chicago Blackhawks (17-31-6, in the Central Division) vs. St. Louis Blues (24-26-5, in the Central Division) St. Louis; Saturday, 7 p.m. EST BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Blues -250, Blackhawks +203; over/under is 5.5 BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks visit the St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Shaun King and the Bucs’ Super Bowl XXXVII Victory: A Different View
item.description: Shaun King was at Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego, watching his team, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, dominate Super Bowl XXXVII. It was surreal. Just a few seasons ago, he had been under center, leading this franchise through tough playoff battles.
item.dc:creator: Rod Carter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NCAA changes participation policy on transgender athletes
item.description: The NCAA has changed its participation policy for athletes, keeping transgender students from women's sports
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Madsen brothers lead Utes past Colorado, 72-59
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) - Gabe Madsen scored 17 points, Lawson Lovering scored 12 points and Utah kept Colorado winless in the Big 12 beating the Buffaloes 72-59 on Wednesday night. Ezra Ausar and reserve Mason Madsen each scored 10 points for the Utes (13-9, 5-6 Big 12).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: A huge National Signing Day: Coverage from six schools as 14 athletes sign
item.description: KREX Sports Anchors Big Will Levenson and Titus Cleveland have coverage from six different schools on a busy day.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Several student-athletes lock in colleges on first NSD of 2025
item.description: Sean Lewis made it clear he wanted to put a fence around San Diego in the recruiting world. He proved it in the high school realm by bringing in 2024 Iron Hog winner Jett Thomas of La Jolla.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to sign order banning trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The executive order will ban people biologically assigned males at birth from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement creates $5 million fund for NWSL players after abuse scandal
item.description: The National Women's Soccer League will establish a $5 million fund for players as part of a settlement that stemmed from allegations of emotional and sexual misconduct that rocked the league in 2021. Attorneys general from Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: Tri City Christian 49, Army Navy 37
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great game on Monday night in Vista with Tri City Christian hosting Army Navy. All tied up at 21 at halftime, the Eagles pulled off and won 49-37. Lead by Caleb Williams who hit for 14. Army Navy was lead by Yanler Ramos, he also poured in 14.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers legend Steve Young believes football was made for girls
item.description: ATHERTON, Calif. - Steve Young is fully invested in one of Silicon Valley's newest startups - girls flag football. Whether it's the size of the field. "The front line you don't see. You see the cone here? Every first down is a cone." The size of the ball.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: Fans at Raptors game continue trend of booing US national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Fans at a Toronto Raptors game continued an emerging trend Sunday of booing the American national anthem at pro sporting events in Canada.
item.dc:creator: IAN HARRISON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chase Elliott wins Clash at Bowman Gray and sets sights on following it with Daytona 500 victory
item.description: NASCAR returned to its roots with a fight-free preseason exhibition at Bowman Gray Stadium and a popular victory with Chase Elliott winning The Clash on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drew Brees serves up pickleball to beloved New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS - Drew Brees had his fair share of fun in New Orleans, taking the Saints to football heaven. Now. the future Hall of Famers found a second line of work, quarterbacking one of the fastest growing sports in America. "Look. I'm a sports junkie, right? I love ping pong. I love tennis.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9
